I keep that around to remind me what I'm dealing with. Okay. Begin by being known for something. You want to like settle on one great competency, one great characteristic, one great story, one great thing about you that you are going to leverage everything else off of. So like in your case it was, you know, make a whole lot more money in a mortgage business, work less, deal with a certain type of customer. And then you start to fuzzy it, you start to fuzzy it, you start to fuzzy it, but you need to pick something. Almost everybody goes broad to narrow, narrow to broad, broad to narrow. It may be easier to look at this in your notebook than it is to look up out of the screen. My old joke itself is explanatory, so we'll just move on. But so there, a lot of people go broad to narrow, back out to broad. They start narrow, they go narrow to broad. Actually during the length of your career you'll be moving from one to the other and back again and in and out. This is just sort of a quick little chart of me. And so at the very beginning, very broad. I mean I started back with, hey, here's direct marketing for any business and any sales guy. It doesn't matter what you do, it doesn't matter what business you're in, it doesn't matter what the size is, I'm going to give you better marketing. Then it got a little narrower to magnetic marketing, but mostly what I was known for for almost a decade, if you acquist people about it, it would be the three step mail campaign and then it would be long copy. Well those are both things pretty narrow. Then get broad again and get even broader, fuzzy the edges, start to be talking about entrepreneurial strategies, success strategies and so forth. Now from there back narrow again starting to develop the marketing to the affluent category, which is at least as narrow as magnetic marketing was. I've been market niched, I've been non-market niched, gone from narrow to broad, from broad to narrow, you will do the same thing. If you look at, everybody's known for one thing and then they fuzzy the edges. So if you look at our friend Trump for example, he actually started pretty much being viewed as a negotiation guy under real estate. Now he's fuzzy the edges to wealth success strategies in general. Now he's fuzzy the edges to lifestyle issues in general. Dale Carnegie began purely about how to stand up and give the speech without throwing out. That was Dale Carnegie's position in the marketplace. And so all their corporate clients, as soon as somebody got to the place in their corporate career that they had to be able to do a group presentation, they sent him to Dale Carnegie. Then Dale Carnegie leveraged it, got fuzzier and broader, got into sales, got into leadership. Dr. Phil is kind of funny I think actually, but I still find him doing weight loss amusing. But a quick example of can anybody do it. Here's a, I just pulled this out of DM news. There's a guy that is an executive recruiter in the direct marketing industry, so meaning companies hire him to go find me a VP of this, thus or so. Now he's started a new coaching business for direct marketers seeking a job. So I was going to take money from the other side. Now of course he doesn't know any better. So let's see here.
